qa_pairs.question: What happens if an Abaqus job submitted on an Alliance cluster does not have enough license tokens available?
qa_pairs.answer: If an Abaqus job submitted on an Alliance cluster does not have enough license tokens available, it will enter an idle "queued" state on a compute node, waiting for the necessary licenses to become available.

qa_pairs.question: What are the negative consequences when an Abaqus job enters an idle "queued" state on an Alliance cluster due to a lack of license tokens?
qa_pairs.answer: When an Abaqus job enters an idle "queued" state on an Alliance cluster because of insufficient license tokens, it wastes available computing resources and depreciates the user's fair share level, as the job consumes CPU time in terms of account priority even though it is not actively computing.
